why_lost: Bought the next open after a parabolic blowoff bar (low wicked SMA at +6% but closed +18% over a rising SMA, 60-pt range); the 10% stop was mean-reversion distance and the spike unwound, MAE -16% in 2 weeks.
earlier_entry: 2024-02-12 wicked the SMA (low +0.8%) but slope 2.3%<3% blocked it and that bar too closed +20% extended; whole Jan-Feb move parabolic, no low-risk pullback existed.
lesson: Reject signals whose close is >10% above the SMA even if the low wicks the band - that is a blowoff, not a pullback.

why_lost: Bought a one-week V-bounce (2130->2319, closed +13% over SMA) off the 44-SMA after a steep correction in a vertical name (slope 23%/13wk); bounce rolled back over, stopped in 3 weeks.
earlier_entry: None clean - prior touch weeks 15-17% extended (Aug-11/18) or the one real pullback (Aug-25) failed green+RS.
lesson: An SMA slope above ~20%/13wk marks a vertical name whose pullbacks are deep and bounces fail - down-weight parabolic slopes.

why_lost: Extreme blowoff entry (range 368-481, closed +25% over SMA); worked 6 weeks (+10%) then COVID gapped through the wide 368 stop, filling 285 for -2R.
earlier_entry: 2020-01-06/01-13 touched cleanly (low +2.5%/-1.6%) but RS below 40wk so both blocked; RS-confirmed ~366 entry would still be hit by COVID but far less extended.
lesson: Cap risk_pct - a 17.8% stop invites >1R losses on any gap; blowoff entries carry unavoidably wide stops.

why_lost: Bought the blowoff week (1521->1801, +18% in one week) at next open 1771, 23.5% over SMA; vertical spike mean-reverted (MAE -10.9% in 2wk) and stopped.
earlier_entry: Yes - 2025-05-26 clean touch (low +1.3%, close +6.7%) fired with entry ~1521/stop ~1440 (~5% risk) at the SMA; triggered a week before the blowoff entry.
lesson: When a clean touch fired the prior week, do not re-enter the following blowoff week 20%+ extended - the setup already triggered.

why_lost: Next open after a wide V-reversal bar (318->361, closed +16% over SMA) that followed a break BELOW the SMA the prior week; bounce stalled, chopped back to the 318 stop over 4 weeks.
earlier_entry: 2022-11-14 touched (low -2.1%) but red + RS-below; before that 15-30% extended all Oct. Only a break-and-bounce, no trend pullback.
lesson: A touch manufactured by a prior-week break-and-bounce is a whipsaw setup, not a trend pullback.

why_lost: Exhaustion blowoff week (897->1051, +12%) at next open 1006, 19.5% over SMA, into the Jan-2025 selloff; MAE -21% through the 890 stop.
earlier_entry: Yes - based 780-890 above a rising SMA for months; 2024-11-25 clean touch (low +1.3%, close +6.4%) entry ~889/stop ~846 (~5% risk); first base-touch passed over for the later blowoff.
lesson: Prefer the first clean touch in a base; skip the later exhaustion blowoff even when it passes every filter.

why_lost: Wide blowoff bar (193-233, +21% over a steep SMA, slope 23%) at 22.7% extension; never trended, oscillated the full 13-week cap, timed out near flat (MAE -17.8% but survived stop).
earlier_entry: 2018-10-08 touched (low +3.0%) but red; 10-01 green but no touch. Cleaner ~197-209 entry existed but never green+touch on one bar; likely similar time-out with less extension.
lesson: Extended blowoff entries that survive the stop still bleed out via the 13-week cap - extension predicts chop, not only stop-outs.

why_lost: Huge-range bounce bar (1078-1328, +23% intraweek, closed +19% over SMA) after unwinding from a 50%-extended peak; bounce failed, drifted back through 1078 over 11 weeks for -1R.
earlier_entry: 2025-01-13 cleaner touch (low +6.7%, close +10.8%) but the name was rolling over from deep extension - an earlier entry likely also lost.
lesson: Wide-range bounce bars closing ~20% over the SMA are exhaustion, especially after a name unwinds from deep extension.

why_lost: Rallied +15.6% MFE but never reached the far +2R target (2R=22% because the stop was 11% wide), round-tripped and timed out flat; a giveback amplified by a stop so wide the half-book was unreachable.
earlier_entry: 2022-03-21 (low +2.2%) during the Feb-Mar bottom was far better: entry ~2704/stop ~2686 would catch the whole run to 3579 and bank the +2R half.
lesson: A wide stop pushes the +2R half-book out of reach - cap risk so a 15% pop banks partial profit before the round-trip.

why_lost: Signal-week 'touch' rests on a weekly low of 274 while the bar opened 347/closed 351/high 357 and both neighbour weeks lowed 340+ - a bad-tick low that manufactured a phantom pullback; entry 35% over SMA, 21.6% stop, bled to a -0.53R time exit.
earlier_entry: None valid - ran 33-38% above the SMA for two months, never pulled back; strip the bad-tick low and this week never touches.
lesson: Reject a touch created by a single-week spike-low sitting far below the same bar's open/close and neighbours' lows - validate the weekly low against daily bars.

why_lost: Blowoff week (565->657, +21% range) at next open 661, 24% over SMA; spiked once more to 746 (MFE +12.8%) then reversed and crashed through the 565 stop within 6 weeks.
earlier_entry: 2021-09-20/09-27 touched the clean base (low -3.6%/+1.3%, ~569-578) but RS below 40wk; RS only crossed on the blowoff week - a lagging-RS timing trap.
lesson: RS confirming only on the blowoff week is a timing trap - the clean base entry gets skipped precisely because RS lags price.

why_lost: ~16 consecutive weeks of identical flat OHLC (300.9, an illiquid/non-trading fill) poison the 44-SMA and slope; the first real bar (09-25) spiked 291->337 and the entry bought that blowoff at 20% extension, stopping in 7 weeks.
earlier_entry: None valid - prior weeks were flat synthetic prints with no real touch; first genuine bar (09-18) already +22% over SMA.
lesson: Guard against dead/flat OHLC runs - a 44-SMA built on carried prices manufactures false slope and false touches.

why_lost: Weakest-slope name (8%); entry on a wide indecision bar (low pierced SMA to -5.5%, close +10.7%) gave a 14.7% stop from the deep wick, then chopped 128-143 for 13 weeks, timing out at -0.02R.
earlier_entry: 2018-04-23 marginally cleaner touch (low -1.3%, close +7.8%, entry ~137.6/stop 124.3), same dead range - likely also a time-out scratch.
lesson: Weakest-slope names in tight ranges produce time-out scratches, and a deep-wick low inflates the stop and wrecks R:R.
